explanations.1: The prelude explains that if we define sound as a physical disturbance transmitted through matter, it exists regardless of whether someone is there to perceive it.
explanations.2: Sound waves in fluids are longitudinal because fluids cannot provide the restoring force necessary for shear or transverse motion.
explanations.3: Section 17.1 defines sound as a disturbance of atoms that is far more ordered than their random thermal motions.
explanations.4: As a wave spreads out from a source, its energy is distributed over an increasing area, leading to a decrease in amplitude.
explanations.5: Pitch is related to frequency and wavelength ($v=f\lambda$); high pitch corresponds to short wavelengths, which are produced by smaller instruments.
explanations.6: Using the formula $v_w = (331\text{ m/s})\sqrt{T/273\text{ K}}$, for $303\text{ K}$, the speed is approximately $349\text{ m/s}$.
explanations.7: Sound travels fastest in solids because they are more rigid and less compressible than liquids or gases.
explanations.8: The frequency of a sound wave is determined by its source and remains constant even when the speed and wavelength change as it enters a new medium.
explanations.9: Intensity is defined as the power per unit area carried by a wave, measured in Watts per square meter.
explanations.10: Because the decibel scale is logarithmic, each factor of 10 in intensity corresponds to an increase of $10\text{ dB}$.
explanations.11: A $3\text{ dB}$ increase in sound intensity level corresponds to a doubling of the physical intensity ($I$).
explanations.12: The intensity of a sound wave is related to the square of its pressure amplitude, as shown in the formula $I = (\Delta p)^2 / (2\rho v_w)$.
explanations.13: Due to the Doppler effect, motion toward the observer causes the waves to arrive more frequently, resulting in a higher observed pitch.
explanations.14: Using $f_{obs} = f_s(v_w / (v_w - v_s))$, we get $150(340 / (340 - 35)) \approx 167\text{ Hz}$.
explanations.15: An aircraft creates two conical shock waves, one at the front (nose) and one at the rear (tail), which are heard as two booms.
explanations.16: A sonic boom is the constructive interference of sound created by an object moving faster than the speed of sound.
explanations.17: Active noise reduction uses a microphone to analyze noise and a speaker to produce an anti-phase wave that cancels the noise via destructive interference.
explanations.18: Air movement is halted at the closed end (node) and is free to move at its maximum at the open end (antinode).
explanations.19: The fundamental frequency for an open tube is $v_w/2L$, whereas for a closed tube it is $v_w/4L$, making the open tube's fundamental twice as high.
explanations.20: For a tube closed at one end, $L = v_w / (4f_1)$. Thus, $344 / (4 \times 128) = 0.672\text{ m}$.
explanations.21: Frequencies below $20\text{ Hz}$ are infrasound and above $20,000\text{ Hz}$ are ultrasound, both typically outside the human range.
explanations.22: Timbre, or tone quality, is determined by the number and relative intensity of multiple frequencies (overtones) present in the sound.
explanations.23: Phons take into account that human ears are more sensitive to certain frequencies than others, even at the same physical decibel level.
explanations.24: The middle ear amplifies the pressure waves by approximately 40 times to efficiently transmit vibrations from the air to the fluid-filled inner ear.
explanations.25: High-intensity ultrasound ($10^3$ to $10^4\text{ W/m}^2$) is converted to thermal energy to treat overworked muscles.
explanations.26: Acoustic impedance is defined as $Z = \rho v$, where $\rho$ is the density and $v$ is the speed of sound in the medium.
explanations.28: The rule of thumb for penetration limit is about $500\lambda$; higher frequencies provide more detail but cannot penetrate as deeply.
explanations.29: When the hair cells (cilia) are damaged but nerves are functional, cochlear implants use electrodes to emulate the cilia's function.
explanations.30: The total distance traveled is $1540 \times 1.2 = 1848\text{ m}$, but since this is an echo, the object distance is half of that ($924\text{ m}$).
